mnemonics.title: FBO Creation Steps
mnemonics.content: D-G-C-B-C-A: Delete(Gen) -> Generate -> Bind -> Configure -> Check -> Attach/Use

mnemonics.title: Framebuffer Completeness Rules
mnemonics.content: CARS: Color attachments must have images, Attachments complete, Resources valid, Same samples for all

mnemonics.title: Attachment Types
mnemonics.content: CDS - Color for display, Depth for Z-testing, Stencil for masking

acronyms.usage: GPU memory buffer for efficient depth/stencil storage
Rendering to texture via FBO for post-processing
Technique to smooth edges using multiple samples per pixel in framebuffer
Synchronizes framebuffer swap with monitor refresh rate

visualTips.visualization: Think of FBO as a picture frame holding multiple sheets (attachments) - color sheet, depth sheet, stencil sheet. Only complete frames can be displayed.
Imagine two conveyor belts - one showing to audience (front), one being painted (back). Swap when painting complete.
Circular queue of buffers. Front buffer displayed, back buffer drawn, then rotate positions.

analogies.analogy: Like a photo album with designated pages for different types of photos - some for color prints, some for black & white (depth), some for polaroid overlays (stencil).
Renderbuffer is like a printed photo - final product, cannot be edited. Texture is like a digital file - can be modified, sampled, and reused.
Like animators use - draw frames on separate sheets, flip through them to create smooth animation.
